workload_description: Full NMR pipeline for anti-3,4-difluoroheptane: ab initio chemical shieldings via RHF/STO-3G + CPHF, and 19F spectrum simulation via spin Hamiltonian time evolution (expv). Connects quantum chemistry (shieldings_module) with quantum spin dynamics (expv) on a single platform, demonstrating Pareto frontier scaling from 7 to 59 basis functions (chemistry) and 3 to 5+ spins (dynamics).
justification: For shieldings, cpu+gpu is 30x faster than cpu-only (752K vs 22.6M tu) with zero error and 0.31g CO2. The cpu+gpu+qpu option saves only 12% more time while introducing 10.28% error and 103kg CO2 — a 330,000x carbon penalty. For spin dynamics (5-spin), cpu+gpu at 383 tu is 21x faster than cpu-only (8196 tu) with zero error. The QPU-hybrid option has 73.5% error, unusable for NMR. At current system sizes, GPU acceleration dominates; the quantum crossover requires larger spin counts where 2^n scaling favors native quantum evolution.
baseline_comparison: PySCF RHF/STO-3G for H2O shieldings (E=-74.963 Ha, O:1252 ppm, H:329 ppm). scipy.linalg.expm for spin dynamics FID (256 points, 5-spin subsystem). Relative error <1e-4 for H2O vs PySCF reference. SPINACH ground truth available for full 16-spin spectrum comparison.
stretch: Phosphorus cluster (34 spins = 2^34 Hilbert space) matching qsim-statevec simulator capacity. Frontier movement analysis across 4 subsystem sizes. Carbon footprint analysis showing 330,000x QPU/GPU ratio.
